data.title: As a developer, large commits are automatically routed to the specialized review path based on file count
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a file-count threshold check near the top of REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md that counts changed files (excluding .test-index and binary/generated files), compares against review.huge_diff_file_threshold (default 20), and diverts to REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md when the threshold is met.

**Why**: Enables all subsequent large-commit review capabilities. Without this gate, no large commits reach the new review path, leaving the broken haiku-routing and 600-line rejection behavior in place for large refactors.

**Scope**:
- IN: threshold check logic, .test-index exclusion from file count, configurable threshold (review.huge_diff_file_threshold), diversion signal to REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md, validation error on 0 or negative threshold values
- OUT: content of REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md (Story S2), configuration documentation (Story S6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a commit with >=20 changed files (excluding .test-index) triggers diversion to REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md, and a commit with fewer than 20 files proceeds through the standard REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md path
  ← Satisfies: "A commit touching >=20 files (excluding .test-index) is automatically diverted from REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md into a new REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md without any manual intervention"
- When this story is complete, .test-index is excluded from the file count before comparing against the threshold
  ← Satisfies: ".test-index is excluded from the file count threshold check"
- When this story is complete, setting review.huge_diff_file_threshold to 0 or a negative integer causes a validation error at threshold-check time rather than silently accepting the value
  ← Satisfies: "Setting 0 or a negative integer is rejected with a validation error at threshold-check time"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Threshold check logic must be testable without running a full review workflow — the file-count and threshold-comparison steps should be extractable as unit-testable functions

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
